items.headline: Ghana awards major gold mine to local company as Africa pushes resource ownership
items.body: Ghana selected Engineers and Planners to operate the Damang gold mine after Gold Fields' lease expired, concluding a tender announced Tuesday that hands control to a company led by the president's brother. The move reflects a broader push to increase local participation in African mining, with similar transitions seen at other mines and across West Africa including Côte d'Ivoire.

items.headline: Nigerian doctors launch indefinite strike over scrapped allowance agreement
items.body: The Nigerian Association of Resident Doctors commenced a nationwide strike withdrawing services across public hospitals after government moved to scrap a professional allowance agreement reached after a 2025 strike. Nigeria already has roughly one doctor for every 5,000 patients in underserved areas where WHO recommends one to 600, and the strike will widen that gap.

items.headline: Cuba releases over 2,000 prisoners as Trump administration tightens pressure
items.body: Cuba's government pardoned 2,010 prisoners during Easter Holy Week following pledges made in March to free prisoners as the United States increases pressure on Cuba's leadership. Human rights groups including Prisoners Defenders and Justicia 11J report no political prisoners were among those released, with over 700 political prisoners remaining behind bars according to these organizations.

items.headline: Australia charges decorated soldier with five war crime murders in Afghanistan
items.body: Ben Roberts-Smith was arrested at Sydney Airport and charged with five counts of war crime murder following a years-long investigation, becoming only the second Australian veteran of the Afghanistan campaign charged with such offences and the most high-profile. Police allege victims were not taking part in hostilities and were detained, unarmed, and under control of Australian Defence Force members when killed.
